kablix.description: A microcontroller simulator (Arduino Uno / Raspberry Pi Pico) right inside VS Code, 100% offline.
kablix.cmd.loadWorkspaceArtifact: Kablix: Load the compiled artifact of the workspace
kablix.welcome:
Arduino Uno / Raspberry Pi Pico simulator, 100% offline.
[▶ Open the simulator](command:kablix.openSimulator)
[📂 Open a project (.projix)](command:kablix.openProject)
[⚙ Compile & run the active file](command:kablix.compileAndRun)
[↑ Load the workspace artifact](command:kablix.loadWorkspaceArtifact)
[📖 Documentation](https://github.com/franksauret/kablix/blob/main/docs/USAGE.en.md)
kablix.config.micropythonUf2: Path (absolute or workspace-relative) of the MicroPython .uf2 firmware used to run .py files on the simulated Pico. Leave empty: Kablix auto-detects a firmware in the workspace, and otherwise offers to download the official firmware on first run.
kablix.config.checkUpdatesOnStartup: Check the npm registry once at startup for newer versions of the bundled simulation libraries. Off by default (Kablix is offline by default); when on, a notification only appears if an update exists. You can also run the check manually with the "Kablix: Check for library updates" command.
kablix.config.checkFirmwareUpdatesOnStar: Check once at startup whether the cached MicroPython firmware (.uf2) for the simulated Pico matches the version bundled with this Kablix release, and offer to upgrade it if not. Off by default; when on, a notification only appears if an upgrade is available. You can also run the upgrade manually with the "Kablix: Upgrade Pico firmware" command.
kablix.config.restoreLastProjectOnStartu: Reopen the last Kablix project (.projix) that was open when VS Code closed, in the simulator, on startup. On by default. Other text tabs (including the associated code file) are restored natively by VS Code.
kablix.config.arduinoCliPath: Full path to the arduino-cli executable (or the folder containing it). Set this if Kablix reports arduino-cli as missing even though it is installed (e.g. it is not on the PATH seen by VS Code). Leave empty to auto-detect via the PATH and common install locations.
kablix.config.toolchainPath: Extra folder searched for the compilation tools (arduino-cli, avr-gcc, arm-none-eabi-gcc, objcopy, objdump). Useful for a portable toolchain that is not on the PATH. Leave empty to use the PATH only.
kablix.config.showLoadBinaryButton: Show the "Load binary" button in the simulator toolbar (loads an already-compiled .hex/.uf2 from the workspace without recompiling). Hidden by default.
kablix.config.picowNetworkBridge: Pico W: let MicroPython scripts make REAL HTTP requests (network/urequests) tunneled through the host (the Wi-Fi chip is not emulated). The host performs the actual fetch and returns the response to the script. On by default; turn off to block all outbound requests from simulated scripts.
kablix.config.selectionColor: Selection color (parts, wires, bends). Any #rrggbb hex color. Kept vivid by default so it stays visible on all themes.
